name: Ghost-intheShell
summary: Ghost Shell is a secure, stealthy shell implementation in Rust designed for privacy and low-profile operations. It features process masking, secure memory handling, and built-in "ghost" commands for covert utilities.

documentation.readme: Ghost Shell is a secure, stealthy shell implementation in Rust designed for privacy and low-profile operations. It features process masking, secure memory handling, and built-in "ghost" commands for covert utilities.
usage_sections: Process Masking (Linux): Automatically disguises the process name as systemd-journald upon initialization to blend in with system processes.
Secure Memory: Utilizes the zeroize crate to ensure input buffers and sensitive data are scrubbed from memory when dropped.
Volatile History: Command history is kept strictly in RAM and is never written to disk (.bashhistory etc.), ensuring no forensic trace remains after exit.
Ghost Commands (::): A set of internal, prefixed commands that never touch the underlying system shell history.
Clipboard Injection: Securely copy text to the system clipboard directly from the shell without trace files.

examples: GHOST MODE ACTIVE. MEMORY SECURE. TRACE: NONE.
DATA INJECTED TO CLIPBOARD. TRACES REMOVED.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone git@github.com:ind4skylivey/Ghost-intheShell.git. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cargo build --release`. Further invocations from upstream: `cargo run --release`; `./target/release/ghost-shell`. Ghost Shell is a secure, stealthy shell implementation in Rust designed for privacy and low-profile operations. It features process masking, secure memory handling, and built-in "ghost" commands for covert utilities.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: Ghost Shell is a secure, stealthy shell implementation in Rust designed for privacy and low-profile operations. It features process masking, secure memory handling, and built-in "ghost" commands for covert utilities.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Ghost Shell is a secure, stealthy shell implementation in Rust designed for privacy and low-profile operations. It features process masking, secure memory handling, and built-in "ghost" commands for covert utilities.
